role: Government spokesperson (2005–2009); Justice Minister (2007–2009); Guardian Council member and spokesman
bio:
Gholam-Hossein Elham is an Iranian jurist and politician who held multiple roles in [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s government. He served as government spokesperson from 2005, and in February 2007 was narrowly confirmed by the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) as [Minister of Justice](/orgs/ministry-of-justice), holding both posts simultaneously until 2009.

Prior to his cabinet roles, Elham was a member of the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) from 2003 to 2008 and served as the council's spokesman. He holds a PhD in criminology from [Tarbiat Modares University](/orgs/tarbiat-modares-university).

Elham was a vocal defender of Ahmadinejad's policies and dismissed allegations of electoral fraud in the [2009 presidential election](/events/presidential-election-iran-2009).

sources.title: Iranian Parliament Approves New Justice Minister

editorial_notes: Born 1959. Guardian Council member 2003-2008 and its spokesman. PhD in criminology from Tarbiat Modares. User brief says VP 2012-13 but not confirmed in web sources — omitted for now.
